command: nebius compute instance create --name stead-ruby-asr-finetune --resources-platform gpu-h100-sxm --resources-preset 1gpu-16vcpu-200gb --boot-disk-image-family ubuntu24.04-cuda13.0 --boot-disk-size 200GiB --cloud-init-user-data-file C:\Users\trico\Documents\GitHub\build-day\model\artifacts\cloud_init.yaml --network-interfaces [{"subnet_id": "<NEBIUS_SUBNET_ID>", "ip_address": {}, "public_ip_address": {}}]

post_create.1_get_ip: export IP=$(nebius compute instance get --name stead-ruby-asr-finetune --format json | jq -r '.status.network_interfaces[0].public_ip_address.address' | cut -d/ -f1)
post_create.3_train: ssh ruby@$IP "pip install -q 'transformers>=4.44' datasets accelerate peft evaluate jiwer soundfile librosa torch && python ~/stead/scripts/train_whisper.py --train ~/stead/train.jsonl --config ~/stead/finetune_config.json --out ~/stead/ckpt"

note: H100 VM path (default). The trainer is scripts/train_whisper.py (standard HF Whisper LoRA fine-tune). It writes a LoRA adapter to ckpt/; eval.py loads it as the fine-tuned model. CLI shape: https://docs.nebius.com/compute/quickstart
